mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) signal a weakening construction sector and broader economic slowdown, leading to negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the housing market downturn propagates through the economy.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST declines for 3 consecutive months, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment (PAYEMS) indicates a strong labor market, boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward SPX returns is less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at extremely low levels (e.g., below 0.5%) signals extreme monetary accommodation, which may lead to short-term risk-on behavior and positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If in weeks when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, the 5-day forward return is negative in more than 60% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below the year-over-year CPI inflation rate (i.e., negative real interest rates), it reduces the opportunity cost of holding equities and encourages risk-taking, leading to positive forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If when real rates are negative, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls for two consecutive months indicates a broad labor-demand slowdown, foreshadowing weaker economic activity and negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If after a 2-month simultaneous decline in HOUST and PAYEMS, the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth is a procyclical indicator, but its predictive power flips in late-cycle expansions: high growth (above 4% YoY) signals rising capacity constraints and impending slowdown, leading to negative returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If when INDPRO growth >4% in late-cycle regime, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, supporting corporate revenues and positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward SPX returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (above 3% annualized) signals a healthy economy, leading to positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If quarters with GDP growth >3% are followed by positive 60-day returns in less than 60% of cases, reject.
